source_report: Antenna supply chain intelligence for the UAS/drone industry

antenna_manufacturers.name: TrueRC Canada
antenna_manufacturers.market_share_note: Largest FPV antenna manufacturer — 24% of Forge DB
antenna_manufacturers.manufacturing: Designed and manufactured in Canada. Patented helical antenna technology (US Pat 10,804,618, Can Pat 2968566).
antenna_manufacturers.strategic_significance: Allied-manufactured antenna leader. Canadian production with US/Canadian patents. Genuine NDAA-compliant.

antenna_manufacturers.name: Lumenier (GetFPV)
antenna_manufacturers.ndaa_note: US brand. Some antennas co-developed with TrueRC. Manufacturing origin varies — some may be Chinese CM.
antenna_manufacturers.market_share_note: Second-largest — 23%. House brand of GetFPV, largest US FPV retailer.

antenna_manufacturers.name: Video Aerial Systems (VAS)
antenna_manufacturers.note: US antenna manufacturer. Well-regarded for helical and patch designs.

antenna_manufacturers.name: Team BlackSheep
antenna_manufacturers.note: Crossfire and Tracer antennas. Immortal T dipole is an industry standard.

antenna_technology.summary: FPV antennas are passive RF components. No active electronics, no firmware, no MCU. Manufacturing requires RF engineering expertise and precision fabrication (PCB etching, injection molding, SMA connectors).

omni.note: Omnidirectional — used on drones. 360° coverage. Circular polarized (LHCP/RHCP).

patch.note: Directional patch — used on goggles. Higher gain, narrower beam.

stubby.note: Compact omni — popular for goggle mounting.

dipole.note: Simple linear polarized — used for ELRS receivers (Immortal T).

key_components.pcb: Antenna PCBs use standard FR4 or Rogers high-frequency laminate. Available from allied sources.
key_components.connectors: SMA/RP-SMA/MMCX/U.FL connectors — manufactured by Amphenol (USA), TE Connectivity (Switzerland), Chinese sources.
key_components.cable: Coaxial cable — standard component, allied sources available.

ndaa_antenna_assessment.status: STRONG — Antennas are the MOST NDAA-compliant component category
ndaa_antenna_assessment.detail: 53% of FPV antennas are US-branded (TrueRC/Canada + Lumenier + VAS + XILO). Antennas are passive RF components with no electronics, firmware, or Chinese silicon dependency. Manufacturing is precision fabrication, not semiconductor processing.
ndaa_antenna_assessment.truerc_advantage: TrueRC (Canada) is the gold standard — designed and manufactured in Canada with US/Canadian patents. Proven in military/industrial/security applications.
ndaa_antenna_assessment.realistic_assessment: Antennas should NOT be a supply chain concern for NDAA builds. Allied manufacturing is mature and cost-competitive. This is a solved problem.
ndaa_antenna_assessment.caveat: Some US-branded antennas (Lumenier, XILO) may use Chinese CM for lower-cost models. TrueRC and VAS are the most clearly allied-manufactured.
